channel.title: Tech Insights
channel.description: Daily curated news on AI, developer tools, and the tech industry

item.title: When pandas runs out of RAM, I change one import line
item.description: You know the moment. You've got a Parquet file or a fat CSV, you write the obvious pd.read_parquet(...), and Python sits there. Then either it finishes in its own sweet time, or you get the...

item.title: Running Local LLMs for Coding: No API Keys, Full Control
item.description: Running Local LLMs for Coding: No API Keys, Full Control You've probably noticed the code completion tools getting slower and more rate-limited. You've also probably gotten tired of explaining...

item.title: How to move from an LLM demo to a production-ready healthcare AI agent
item.description: From LLM Demo to Healthcare AI Agent: What Developers Need to Build Around the Model Building an AI agent demo is easy. Building a healthcare AI agent that can survive production is a different...

item.title: 8BitDo’s all-button controller gets a screen upgrade for customizations without an app
item.description: 8BitDo announced a new version of its all-button Arcade Controller that debuted a little over a year ago. The new Arcade Controller Pro looks similar to 8BitDo's original with a leverless (or...

item.title: Zoox’s purpose-built robotaxi is getting a refresh
item.description: Zoox, the autonomous vehicle company owned by Amazon, unveiled a new look for its boxy, bidirectional robotaxi, calling it the "next evolution" of the vehicle intended for mass production. The...

item.title: Build Your Own Modbus-TCP Cache Proxy in Python: One Inverter, Many Home Assistant Clients
item.description: The Huawei SUN2000 SDongle has an annoying trait you only trip over once you want to connect more than one device: it accepts exactly one concurrent Modbus-TCP connection. The moment Home...

item.title: Apache Data Lakehouse Weekly: June 17 to June 24, 2026
item.description: This week the lakehouse community spent most of its energy on two questions. The first is how the catalog grows from a table directory into a control plane that serves agents, BI tools, and query...

item.title: Why Strong Developers Outside the US Get Ignored by American Companies (It's Not Your Code)
item.description: You can build distributed systems, contribute to open source, and pass any technical screen thrown at you. But your applications to US companies go nowhere. No reply. Maybe a form rejection three...

item.title: From Classroom to Real-World: Surviving Week 1 of My Software Engineering Internship
item.description: Week 1 of My Software Engineering Internship This week gave me a reality check. I went from building side projects where I made all the decisions to working on a real team, attending client...

item.title: # How I Built an AI Travel Planner — and What I Got Wrong
item.description: I built Kartografer — an AI travel planner where you describe a trip and Gemini generates a full day-wise itinerary. You can then edit it, chat with the AI for suggestions, share a public link,...

item.title: OpenAI unveils its first custom chip, built by Broadcom
item.description: Named Jalapeño, the new processor was designed specifically for the unique needs of OpenAI's inference systems.

item.title: RAG Explained: Give an LLM Your Own Knowledge
item.description: An LLM doesn't know your company's docs, last week's news, or your private data — and it'll confidently make things up. RAG fixes that: retrieve the relevant facts, put them in the prompt, then...

item.title: REST Brain vs Procedure Brain
item.description: REST Brain vs Procedure Brain Today I had one of the most valuable architecture discussions I've been part of. I joined a monorepo expecting the usual: Frontend → API Server → Database Instead, I...

item.title: OpenAI reveals its first AI processor: Jalapeño
item.description: OpenAI has just revealed a new "intelligence processor" chip for AI servers made in partnership with Broadcom. The chip, called Jalapeño, is designed to power current and future large language...

item.title: This year’s Prime Day deals on Apple products are the best I’ve seen
item.description: Amazon’s Prime Day is now in its second day, and whether you’re looking for a new pair of wireless earbuds or a smartwatch, there’s a good chance you’ll find a discount. The Apple Watch Series 11...

item.title: Laptop prices suck these days, but here are 25 Prime Day deals that help a little
item.description: The RAM, storage, and component crunch has come for new laptop prices. Plenty of models have gone up in price after being on the market for months, and so have those that’ve been on shelves for...

item.title: Philips Hue’s smart lights are getting a connectivity upgrade
item.description: Philips Hue has announced a connectivity upgrade for its Matter-over-Thread-enabled smart bulbs that will allow them to run both Thread and Zigbee at the same time. The change means you can...

item.title: 3 days left to save up to $190 on your TechCrunch Founder Summit 2026 pass
item.description: You have just 3 days left to save up to $190 on your pass to TechCrunch Founder Summit 2026 before Early Bird rates end on June 26 at 11:59 p.m. PT. Register here.

item.title: 19 great Prime Day deals on our favorite charging gear
item.description: If you’re sick of your devices running out of juice early into the evening just as you’re about to go out, Prime Day is a great time to pick up a new power bank or charging brick. We’ve rounded up...

item.title: Google’s TV Streamer 4K is $25 off, its best price ever
item.description: The Google TV Streamer 4K is more than just a terrific streaming device. It can also act as a smart home hub, letting you control all major smart home devices directly from your TV. Thanks to...

item.title: 19 Prime Day deals on our favorite Switch 2 accessories
item.description: The Switch 2’s price is set to increase by $50 on September 1st, starting at $499.99, so if you’re purchasing one before that (or you already own one), now’s a good time to also save on...

item.title: The Google Home Speaker sounds good and looks great — but it’s finicky
item.description: Right out of the box, the new Google Home Speaker passed a couple of important tests. Even with the volume at 100 percent and music blaring out of the speaker, it quickly ducked the audio and...

item.title: Cordyceps CI/CD Flaws Expose 300+ GitHub Repositories to Supply-Chain Attacks
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have flagged a new class of CI/CD workflow weakness that allows attackers to hijack workflows and compromise open-source supply chains. The "critical exploitable pattern"...

item.title: Qualcomm Buys Buzzy Chip Startup Modular for Nearly $4 Billion
item.description: Modular, one of the most promising chip software startups of the AI era, heads for a multibillion-dollar exit.

item.title: The Download: introducing the Engineering issue
item.description: This is today’s edition of The Download, our weekday newsletter that provides a daily dose of what’s going on in the world of technology. Introducing: the Engineering issue We can’t fix...

item.title: Stripe, Anthropic and OpenAI are backing an effort to stop respiratory infections
item.description: The common cold comes for us all—often more than once a year. And there is no way to prevent it. The best you can do is take vitamin C and stay away from people with the sniffles. Now, the payment...

item.title: The emergence of the web data infrastructure layer for AI
item.description: AI is booming. New use cases are emerging each day. To capitalize on the technology’s potential, enterprises require data at scale. In many cases, though, the relevant information is blocked or...

item.title: Dawn of the Apex Agentic Adversary
item.description: We are standing at the end of an era we never thought to mourn: the era of human-speed threats. For years, cybersecurity moved to a rhythm organizations could follow. A researcher found a bug, a...

item.title: Embedding Forbidden Text in Spyware to Discourage AI Analysis
item.description: At least one malware developer is adding text about nuclear and biological weapons to their spyware, in an effort to stop automatic AI analysis. Details: The _index.js payload begins with a large...

item.title: This flying solar-powered platform could deliver better internet from the air
item.description: As soon as August, a giant silver bullet will cut its way through the dry air of the southwestern US and cross the Pacific to reach the coast of Japan. Once there, the roughly 200-foot-long craft,...
